scope: Pass B full-surface expansion, scoped to the organization/network/device/admin INVENTORY-AND-ADMINISTRATION surface this connector already targets (verified against the real Meraki OpenAPI 3.0.1 spec fetched from https://raw.githubusercontent.com/meraki/openapi/master/openapi/spec3.json on 2026-07-04, 670 paths / 957 endpoint-methods total). Covered: 11 read streams (organizations, organization_networks, organization_devices, organization_admins, organization_licenses, organization_config_templates, organization_policy_objects, organization_branding_policies, organization_saml_roles, organization_configuration_changes, organization_api_requests) and 13 write actions covering network/device/admin/configuration-template/policy-object lifecycle mutations. Every endpoint under the org-tier path prefix (/organizations/{organizationId}/...) plus the top-level /organizations/{id}, /networks/{id}, and /devices/{serial} singular resources this connector's fan-out targets is individually enumerated below. The remaining ~900 endpoints under /networks/{networkId}/... and /devices/{serial}/... (per-product-type configuration: wireless SSIDs/RF profiles, switch ports/STP/QoS, appliance firewall/VPN/traffic-shaping, camera video/analytics settings, sensor readings, systems-manager device management, and their organization-tier bulk-query siblings under /organizations/{organizationId}/{wireless|switch|appliance|camera|sensor|sm|...}/*) are a categorically different, vastly larger product surface (an entirely separate configuration domain per network product type) that this organizations/networks/devices/admins-scoped connector was never designed to reach; enumerating each individually here would not change their disposition (all `out_of_scope` for the same reason) and is captured once at this scope level rather than as 900 near-identical entries.

excluded.reason: single-organization detail; identical record shape to the organizations list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream
renames/reconfigures organization-level API-access settings; Pass B breadth-vs-cost triage, not a common inventory-object mutation
permanently deletes an entire Meraki organization and every network/device under it; not a safe reverse-ETL target
provisions a brand-new organization by cloning another; an account-provisioning action outside this connector's per-record write scope

claims physical devices/licenses/orders into an organization's inventory using vendor order/serial identifiers this connector's streams don't originate; a supply-chain action, not a config-object mutation
returns the org's own OpenAPI documentation JSON, not a syncable object
single-network detail; identical record shape to the organization_networks list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream
single-device detail by serial; identical record shape to the organization_devices list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream
single-license detail; identical record shape to the organization_licenses list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream
assigns/moves/renews a license against a device or queues it; a licensing-lifecycle action carrying billing/entitlement implications this connector's writes.json does not model
single-config-template detail; identical record shape to the organization_config_templates list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream
single-policy-object detail; identical record shape to the organization_policy_objects list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream

creates a new Dashboard branding/white-label policy; a low-frequency org-cosmetic configuration action, Pass B breadth-vs-cost triage (this bundle's write scope focuses on inventory objects: networks/devices/admins/templates/policy-objects)
single-branding-policy detail; identical record shape to the organization_branding_policies list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream
same org-cosmetic configuration scope as the create endpoint above
same org-cosmetic configuration scope as the create endpoint above
returns/reorders the evaluation priority order of branding policies, not an independently syncable object; paired with the excluded branding-policy CRUD above
creates an org-tier SAML SSO role granting dashboard access privileges; a security-sensitive access-control action outside this bundle's per-record inventory write scope
single-SAML-role detail; identical record shape to the organization_saml_roles list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream
modifies an org-tier SAML SSO role's access privileges; same security-sensitive scope as SAML role creation
revokes an org-tier SAML SSO role; same security-sensitive scope as SAML role creation
org-wide dashboard login/password-policy settings (a single settings object, not a list/detail resource); Pass B breadth-vs-cost triage
modifies org-wide authentication/password-policy enforcement; a security-sensitive settings mutation outside this bundle's per-record inventory write scope
org-wide SAML-SSO-enabled toggle (a single settings object, not a list/detail resource); Pass B breadth-vs-cost triage
enables/disables org-wide SAML SSO authentication; a security-sensitive settings mutation outside this bundle's per-record inventory write scope
org-wide SNMP monitoring-credentials settings (a single settings object, not a list/detail resource); Pass B breadth-vs-cost triage
writes org-wide SNMP community-string/monitoring credentials; a secret-bearing settings mutation this connector's writes.json avoids
lists asynchronous bulk-action-batch jobs (a create-then-poll async pattern), not a plain inventory object; Pass B breadth-vs-cost triage
creates an asynchronous bulk-action-batch job with no declarative create-then-poll equivalent in this dialect
single-action-batch-job detail; same async-job scope as the action-batches list endpoint above
confirms/updates a pending bulk-action-batch job; same async-job scope as action-batch creation
cancels a pending bulk-action-batch job; same async-job scope as action-batch creation

every per-network-product-type configuration endpoint (wireless SSIDs/RF profiles, switch ports/STP/QoS, appliance firewall/VPN/traffic-shaping, camera video/analytics settings, sensor readings, systems-manager device management, and their organization-tier bulk-query siblings — roughly 900 of the API's 957 total endpoint-methods) is a categorically different, per-product configuration surface this organizations/networks/devices/admins inventory-and-administration connector was never scoped to reach; see this file's top-level scope note for the full rationale
